briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolina rig but only one knot.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, bluffs. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Yama Craw buoyancy makes claws float up behind football jig in defensive crawfish posture. Perfect for bottom-hopping presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, bluffs. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...
Johnston: Uses Garmin LiveScope Perspective Mode to spot individual bed fish in rippled conditions when you can't see them visually. Cast to them and catch on first attempt.
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal spawning bass become extremely lockjaw but won't abandon beds. Fish are still in 2-5ft but much harder to trigger. Slow way down and make repeated presentations to visible beds.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
